raw.title: Meta's months-old AI unit is a soul-crushing gulag, say the engineers stuck inside it
raw.body: Meta's three-month-old Applied AI team of approximately 6,500 engineers is on the verge of revolt. Employees describe it as a 'soul-crushing gulag' where staff are being 'drafted' into the unit involuntarily to generate puzzles for AI training. A livestreamed internal presentation was hijacked by an expletive-laden outburst. Over 1,600 employees signed a petition protesting keystroke and click monitoring for AI training data. Mark Zuckerberg issued an internal memo acknowledging the 'distress' but defending the program as essential for Meta's AI ambitions.

raw.title: Jeff Bezos's Prometheus raises $12B to build an 'artificial general engineer' for the physical world
raw.body: Prometheus, the physical AI startup co-founded by Jeff Bezos, raised $12 billion at a $41 billion valuation. The company aims to build an 'artificial general engineer' that can automate design and manufacturing of complex physical systems including jet engines and drug compounds. The round was backed by JPMorgan Chase, Goldman Sachs, and BlackRock, signaling Wall Street's growing appetite for AI infrastructure bets.

raw.title: Google just fired a warning shot in the AI subscription price wars
raw.body: Google cut the price of Google AI Plus from $7.99/month to $4.99/month while doubling storage to 400GB. This move brings the AI subscription price war that has been brewing in emerging markets like India squarely to U.S. consumers. OpenAI and Anthropic, both having confidentially filed for IPO, face intensifying commoditization pressure as competition drives prices down.

raw.title: Warner Music acquires AI attribution startup Sureel AI
raw.body: Warner Music Group has acquired Sureel AI, a startup that uses 'AI DNA' technology to track when artists' work is used in AI model training. The acquisition highlights the growing tension between the music industry and AI companies over copyright and attribution. Sureel's technology creates a fingerprint for creative works and monitors training datasets for unauthorized usage.

raw.title: Apple's AI runs on Nvidia chips: Inside the Private Cloud Compute reveal at WWDC 2026
raw.body: At WWDC 2026, Apple revealed it worked with Nvidia, Google, and Intel to make Private Cloud Compute work. Apple's Foundation Model runs on Nvidia hardware within Google's cloud infrastructure, marking a significant departure from Apple's traditional preference for in-house silicon. The move signals that even Apple cannot escape Nvidia's dominance in AI compute.

raw.title: ChatGPT hit 1 billion monthly active users faster than any app in history
raw.body: According to Sensor Tower data, ChatGPT reached 1 billion monthly active users, becoming the fastest application to achieve this milestone in history. The milestone underscores the unprecedented speed of AI adoption among consumers. OpenAI Codex also hit 5 million weekly active users with new plugins for building interactive hosted websites.

raw.title: Google signs $920M/month deal with SpaceX for AI compute capacity
raw.body: Google signed a deal to pay SpaceX $920 million per month from October 2026 through June 2029 to meet surging demand for Gemini Enterprise. The deal represents one of the largest cloud compute contracts in history and highlights the extreme demand for AI inference capacity.

raw.title: Microsoft unveils seven self-developed MAI models at Build 2026, ushering in the 'Agent-first' era
raw.body: At Microsoft Build 2026, the company unveiled seven self-developed MAI models including MAI-Thinking-1, its first reasoning model, and MAI-Code-1-Flash for code generation. CEO Satya Nadella declared this the 'Agent-first era' where AI agents become the primary interface for computing. Broadcom projected $560 billion in AI chip revenue for fiscal 2026.
